title: Yee Moh
content:

'''Yee Moh''' was a rather lonely-looking [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian]]<ref name="FF" /> [[Gender/Legends|male]]<ref name="Facebook" /> who lost his job<ref name="SWI147" /> by [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of {{Film|II}} to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]</ref> During a [[Local day/Legends|night]]<ref name="AOTC" /> in that [[Standard year/Legends|year]],<ref name="NEC" /> Moh visited the [[Outlander Club/Legends|Outlander Club]],<ref name="AOTC" /> a [[Nightclub/Legends|nightclub]] and [[Gambling/Legends|gambling]] hall in the [[Uscru Entertainment District/Legends|Uscru Entertainment District]]<ref name="Club">{{CSWECite|II|431|Outlander Club}}</ref> on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Complete Locations">[[Star Wars: Complete Locations (2005)|''Star Wars: Complete Locations'']]</ref> [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]],<ref name="Club" /> and found companionship with the Neimoidian sisters [[Ko Itub|Ko]] and [[Rina Itub]].<ref name="SWI147" /> The trio stood together just as the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] walked past them, searching for the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Zam Wesell/Legends|Zam Wesell]] after her [[Plot to assassinate Senator Amidala/Legends|assassination attempt]] on [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]].<ref name="AOTC" /> 

What Moh was doing at the club was a mystery, as members of his [[Species/Legends|species]] preferred gaudier locations.<ref name="FF" /> Moh had mottled [[Color/Legends|green]] skin, and he wore gray [[Clothing/Legends|robes]] and a hat while at the club.<ref name="AOTC" /> He also wore [[Data goggles/Legends|data goggles]], which provided constant [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] data readouts.<ref name="WhosWho">{{InsiderCite|75|Who's Who in the Outlander Club: It's Ladies' Night}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Remember Tey How, the Neimoidian pilot aboard Episode I's droid control ship—the one wearing the pinched googles and the ''Sleeper''-style mouth comlink? Three of her kind appear in the Outlander standing between Ror Ithh and Achk Med-Beq.|Daniel Wallace|<ref name="WhosWho">{{InsiderCite|75|Who's Who in the Outlander Club: It's Ladies' Night}}</ref>}}
[[File:Ko Itub.png|thumb|right|250px|Ko Itub was identified as a Neimoidian previously identified as Yee Moh.]]
Yee Moh first appeared in the [[2002]] [[prequel trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']]. The Outlander Club scenes featuring Moh, who was portrayed by an uncredited extra,<ref name="AOTC" /> were shot on [[July 17]], [[2000]] at [[Fox Studios, Sydney]].<ref name="Mythmaking">''[[Mythmaking: Behind the Scenes of Attack of the Clones]]''</ref> The characters name was first mentioned in an image in the [[StarWars.com]] article "[[Cantina Roll-Call: Shedding Light on Some Alien Aliases]],"<ref name="Cantina">{{SWArchive|url=episode-iv/bts/article/f20030502/index.html|text=Cantina Roll-Call: Shedding Light on Some Alien Aliases|int=Cantina Roll-Call: Shedding Light on Some Alien Aliases}}</ref> where the name was applied to an image of the character [[Tey How/Legends|Tey How]].<ref name="TeyHow">{{CSWECite|II|64|How, Tey}}</ref> The web article was posted on [[May 2]], [[2003]].<ref name="Cantina" />

In the article "[[Who's Who in the Outlander Club: It's Ladies' Night]]," written by [[Daniel Wallace]] and published in the [[Star Wars Insider 75|seventy-fifth]] issue of ''[[Star Wars Insider]]''<ref name="WhosWho" /> on [[April 6]], [[2004]],<ref name="FaceofEvil">{{SWArchive|url=community/fanclub/mag/news20040315.html|text=The New Face of Evil}}</ref> the author makes a connection between Moh, his companions, and How. The article also identified the Neimoidian wearing a mouthpiece as Moh.<ref name="WhosWho" /> In the [[Star Wars Insider 147|147th]] issue of ''Insider'', the "[[Rogues Gallery (department)|Rogues Gallery]]" department,<ref name="SWI147" /> written by [[Tim Veekhoven]], [[Kevin Beentjes|Kevin Bentjes]], and [[Sander de Lange|Sander De Lange]],<ref name="Facebook" /> identifies Moh as a different Neimoidian without a mouthpiece.<ref name="SWI147" /> A [[January 19]], [[2014]], post by the Star Wars Sourcebooks [[Wikipedia:Facebook|Facebook]] account identifies Ko Itub as the Neimoidian identified as Moh in Wallace's article.<ref name="Facebook" /><ref name="WhosWho" /> This article assumes the "Rogues Gallery" article provides the correct identification of the character, as the article was published on [[January 21]], 2014.<ref name="Titan">{{TitanMagazines|url=t/star-wars-insider/us/147|text=Star Wars Insider Issue #147}}</ref>
